web.title: The Effect of Employee-Oriented Flexible Work on Mental Health
web.description: This systematic review suggests that employee-oriented flexible work may have small beneficial effects on mental health.

web.title: A Meta‐Analysis of Antecedents and Outcomes of Flexible Working ...
web.description: FWAs were significantly associated with beneficial outcomes, such as greater job satisfaction, organisational commitment, job autonomy, life satisfaction.

web.title: Flexible work arrangements and employee health: A meta-analytic ...
web.description: Results demonstrate that FWA are associated with better physical health, reduced absenteeism, and fewer somatic symptoms.

web.title: (PDF) Flexible Work Arrangements and Employee Turnover Intentions
web.description: The main findings of the research are that there are direct positive effects of flexible work arrangements and employee engagement on turnover ...

web.title: [PDF] Flexible work arrangements, work engagement, turnover intentions ...
web.description: Flexible work arrangements (FWAs) are often written into company policies to demonstrate organisational sensitivity to potentially difficult interfaces ...
